title: Rincon Park: Bow & Arrow Art and the Bay Bridge - California Through My Lens
content: Rincon Park in San Francisco is one of those places that would be a huge tourist attraction in virtually any other city but is only a mid-level attraction in San Francisco. Rincon Park is a perfect place to relax on a sunny day in the city, with great views of the Bay Bridge and, of course, the main attraction “Cupid’s Span.” Here is all the information so you can visit the park during your next trip to the city. In San Francisco, I never recommend driving, the city is just tough to navigate and park in, and places are so easy to get to via public transit that it’s not worth it. Rincon Park is located a stone’s throw from the Folsom Muni Stop, so I would recommend just making your way to that stop, then you can cross the street, and you will be at the park. It is a popular spot to run in the city, so you will see a lot of runners there.
